source_pr.title: executor: make IndexMergeReader support reading partition table directly
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #24160 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: executor: make IndexMergeReader support reading partition table directly

### What is changed and how it works?
This PR makes `IndexMergeReader` support reading partition tables directly using the similar way with https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/24197.

The picture below shows how we implement this feature:
1. build `KVReq` for each partition table separately;
2. let `IndexWorker` and `PKWorker` handle these `KVReqs` one by one;
3. delivery partition information to `MergeWorker` and `TableWorker` as well; 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7499936/115856845-91ce5900-a45f-11eb-9617-7b991fac5b60.png)

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- executor: make IndexMergeReader support reading partition table directly

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Asynchronous state transitions need polling/Eventually-style checks. This PR stabilizes it by: fix DATA RACE CI problem.

evidence: evidence: fix DATA RACE CI problem
